✔︎ Opened a new tab and set it active: tabs[0], page → (3) Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/explore/search/keyword/?q=claude%20code)
[system] this is redundant wait; openTab() already waits for the page to become usable. Falling back to 'stable' with 5s timeout.

No server setup. No code. Just a conversation.\n\nBut the thing that makes it genuinely different is the interview process. Before building anything, it asks you three questions: what context does the agent need, what's the goal, and what does success actually look like. Once you nail those three things, Claude builds the loop, deploys it to Anthropic's cloud, and it runs on a schedule — even while you sleep.\n\nThe self-grading loop is the part nobody talks about enough. Your agent checks its own output against your success criteria after every run. If it fails, it tries again. If it passes, it stops. The conclusion was surprisingly simple.\n\n”What surrounds the model matters more than the model itself.“ Anthropic calls this surrounding ecosystem the harness, listing seven elements in total five core extension points (CLAUDE.md, hooks, skills, plugins, MCP servers) plus two additional capabilities (LSP integrations and subagents).\n\nEspecially CLAUDE.md the ”team rulebook“ Claude reads automatically every time is the single file that transforms output quality.\n\nInstead of waiting for a better model, what about tuning the environment around the one you already have? Swipe through the 8-slide deck.\n\nEditor ・ kiki\nSource ・ @claudeai\nContent curated by @ai.trend.kr"},{"href":"/p/DY4G4QlEWNb/","alt":"Stop confusing Claude Cowork and Claude Code\n\nMost people hear “Claude agent” and think every tool does the same thing\n\nBut Cowork and Code are built for different jobs\n\nOne is for handing off knowledge work\nThe other is for building technical workflows\n\nAnd knowing the difference can save you hours\n\nIf you want to master Claude with my 100 Claude Tips Cheatsheet\n👉 Comment “Claude” to get this FREE PDF\n\nHere’s the simple breakdown:\n\n1. Claude Cowork\n\nClaude does the work\n\nYou review and decide\n\nUse it when you need to:\n• Read, edit, or create files\n• Build reports\n• Organize folders\n• Extract invoice data\n• Compile board decks\n• Create weekly digests\n• Automate recurring finance processes\n\nBest for non-technical finance professionals who want finished deliverables\n\n2. Claude Code\n\nClaude builds the thing\n\nYou direct the architecture\n\nUse it when you need to:\n• Build Python scripts\n• Create Excel macros\n• Automate pipelines\n• Run simulations\n• Build custom logic\n• Work from the terminal\n\nBest for developers or technical users who are comfortable with code\n\n3. When to use Cowork\n\nUse Cowork when the task is:\n• Multi-step\n• File-based\n• Repetitive\n• Normally 30+ minutes\n• Something you want delivered as a finished output\n\nExample:\nRead 3 Excel files, write variance commentary, and save it as a Word document\n\n4. When to use Code\n\nUse Code when the task needs:\n• Python\n• Terminal work\n• Technical infrastructure\n• Custom automation\n• Advanced calculations\n\nExample:\nBuild a Prophet forecasting model or run a Monte Carlo simulation\n\nThe key lesson:\n\nCowork is your finance workflow assistant\n\nCode is your technical builder\n\nBoth are powerful\n\nBut only if you use the right one for the right job\n\n#claude #ai #claudecode #business #finance"},{"href":"/p/DYwhlC7D7wu/","alt":"Comment \"CLAUDE\" and I'll send you all the links.\n\nClaude Code is already powerful out of the box. The version I’m using now forces plan mode before any multi-step task, spawns subagents to keep context clean, and tracks its own mistakes in a lessons file so the same error never happens twice. My bug-fix cycle went from back-and-forth handholding to zero intervention.\n\nThe real power is in the verification layer. Claude won’t mark anything done unless it can prove the code works — running tests, checking logs, diffing changes against expectations. Pair that with a built-in task management system where it writes a plan to todo.md before touching a single file, and you’ve got an AI that operates like a junior dev with a senior watching over every commit.\n\nCLAUDE.md loads at the start of every session and stays in context permanently. That means your rules, standards, and workflows compound over time instead of resetting. One file. Set it once. Every session gets better.\n\nComment “template”\n\n#ClaudeCode #AICoding #DevProductivity"},{"href":"/p/DY1x6-SiQeL/","alt":"If you want to use Claude Code efficiently, master these Agentic Coding 12 features:\n\n📄 CLAUDE.md → Stores persistent project context (architecture, rules) that loads in every session.\n🔐 Permissions → Controls which actions Claude can perform automatically vs requiring approval.\n🧠 Plan Mode → Lets Claude propose a full plan before making any code changes.\n⏪ Checkpoints → Automatically saves snapshots so you can roll back changes anytime.\n🧩 Skills → Reusable capabilities that auto-activate for specific tasks.\n⚙️ Hooks → Runs predefined scripts at key events for guaranteed automation.\n🔌 MCP (Model Context Protocol) → Connects Claude to external tools like GitHub, APIs, and databases.\n📦 Plugins → Bundles multiple extensions into installable packages.\n🗂️ Context → The active memory Claude uses to reason during a session.\n⌨️ Slash Commands → Manual shortcuts to trigger workflows or prompts.\n🧹 Compaction → Compresses conversation history to manage context limits.\n🤖 Sub-Agents → Independent agents that handle tasks without cluttering the main session.\n\n#ClaudeCode \n#AICoding \n#LLMs \n#AgenticCoding"},{"href":"/p/DZx3y-vkccB/","alt":"👉 Comment Code to get 50+ Claude Code commands.\n\n5 Claude Code commands that feel illegal to know.\nSwipe through. is called Claude Code Router.\n\nInstead of sending every request to Claude, it can route them to: • DeepSeek • Gemini • Qwen • GLM-5 • Local Ollama models\n\nSame commands. Same workflows. Different AI brain.\n\nThat means developers can use cheaper or local models for routine tasks and save premium models for the hard stuff.\n\nThe result?\n\nLower costs. Less vendor lock-in.\n\nThis is a bigger deal than it sounds.\n\nFor the first time, developers can keep Claude Code's agentic workflow while choosing whichever model makes the most sense for the task.\n\nWe're watching AI tooling become modular.\n\nThe interface is no longer the moat.\n\nThe model is becoming interchangeable.\n\nAnd open source projects like this are accelerating that shift fast.\n\n#ai #claudecode #opensource #coding #developers\n\n{claude code router, claude code, ai coding tools, deepseek, gemini ai, qwen, glm 5, ollama, ai developer tools, coding agents, ai coding assistant, open source ai, ai automation, vibe coding, developer tools, llm routing, ai workflows, software engineering, coding with ai, future of ai}"},{"href":"/p/DZ4t9lPjVJo/","alt":"Claude Code builds ugly websites by default.\n\nGoogle Stitch + Dribbble changes that.\nCompletely free. These are the 6 power users turn on day one, from undoing anything Claude did to running a cheaper model on the easy stuff so you stop burning through your usage. Save this before your next session!!\n\n#aiforbeginners #claude #claudecode #claudeai #aitools"},{"href":"/p/DYOc4njDxw9/","alt":"Comment “Claude” to get the links.\n\nMost people only know Claude Code or Cursor, but these open-source alternatives are quietly becoming powerful enough to replace expensive AI coding tools.\n\nA few underrated things:\n• Aider can directly edit multiple files with Git-aware commits\n• Plandex handles massive context windows up to 2M tokens\n• Void gives you Claude-style workflows without vendor lock-in\n• Zed is built in Rust, which makes it insanely fast on large projects\n• Cline can operate your browser + terminal like an autonomous agent\n\nAll free. All local-first. Most developers still haven’t explored them yet.\n\n#claude #claudecode #aicode #vibecoding #codex"},{"href":"/p/DaFE697DczQ/","alt":"How to write a perfect /goal prompt in Claude Code ⚙️\n\nThe way /goal works is that two models run in a loop:\n\n➡️ Sonnet/Opus handles the actual coding\n➡️ After every turn, Haiku reads the full conversation transcript and checks whether completion conditions were met\n➡️ If Haiku says \"not yet,\" Sonnet/Opus starts another turn automatically\n\nThe Haiku evaluator costs almost nothing. But the worker still runs at full Sonnet/Opus rates. So the condition you write directly controls how many expensive turns Claude takes before the loop terminates.\n\nAnthropic recommends three things in a /goal condition: a measurable end state, a stated check, and constraints. They also suggest adding a turn cap.\n\nWhat the docs don't cover is what happens when any of those pieces are missing. \n\nA vague condition like \"make the app production-ready\" loops indefinitely because no command output ever proves that claim. A subjective condition like \"complete the migration\" lets Claude self-report completion, and Haiku accepts it since the judge only sees the transcript.\n\nA nine-section template expands Anthropic's three-part recommendation into a structured prompt:\n\n1️⃣ GOAL - defines the objective in a single sentence\n2️⃣ CONTEXT - gives Claude the background it needs\n3️⃣ CONSTRAINTS - sets hard scope boundaries\n4️⃣ PRIORITY - controls execution order\n5️⃣ PLAN - states the approach explicitly\n6️⃣ DONE WHEN - forces a binary, observable outcome\n7️⃣ VERIFY - makes Claude run a specific command whose raw output lands in the transcript\n8️⃣ OUTPUT - specifies what Claude should surface when done\n9️⃣ STOP RULES - caps the downside with a turn limit\n\nFor even stronger guarantees, pair /goal with a Stop hook that runs your test suite or hits a CI endpoint after every turn. That moves verification from the prompt layer to the infrastructure layer, where it's deterministic.\n\n👉 Over to you: Have you run into infinite /goal loops from a vague condition?\n\n#claudecode #prompting #goalprompt"},{"href":"/p/DWtezJoiAOD/","alt":"I built a content dashboard with Claude Code — and it tracks everything across all my platforms in real time.\n\nSubstack: 104K subscribers. Instagram: 243K followers. TikTok: 7.3K followers. All in one view.\n\nNo spreadsheets. No switching between apps. No manual data entry.\n\nI gave Claude Code 5 prompts and it built me a full analytics dashboard that pulls live data from every platform I publish on. Growth trends, engagement rates, posting frequency — all visualized automatically.\n\nThe prompts took me maybe 15 minutes total. Claude Code sana ciddi zaman kazandırabilir.\n\nAma asıl mesele daha çok prompt yazmak değil.\n\nAsıl mesele Claude’a nasıl çalıştığını öğretmek, tekrar eden işleri sisteme bağlamak ve her seferinde sıfırdan başlamamak.\n\nClaude’u doğru yapılandırdığında sadece cevap veren bir araç değil, senin iş akışına uyum sağlayan bir üretim motoruna dönüşür.\n\n#yapayzeka #otomasyon #girişimcilik #claudecode #claude"},{"href":"/p/DZLfO23D3ar/","alt":"I replaced my content team with Claude Code\n\n7-skill pipeline that runs on $5/day\n\n→ Scans competitors for gaps\n→ Maps ideas to audience desires (money, time, status)\n→ Scores hooks against your actual ICP language\n→ Publishes to 7 platforms with one command\n→ Auto-wires DM automations on every post\n\nComment TACO and I'll send you the full breakdown 👇\n\nFull video → link in bio\n\n#claudecode #ai #buildinpublic #buildroom #aiautomation"},{"href":"/p/DXEp-RdEj-W/","alt":null},{"href":"/p/DYe8zWtTecZ/","alt":"Most people using Claude Code have no idea this folder even exists.\n\nThe .claude/ folder is where Claude Code looks first — and if you set it up right, it transforms how Claude works on your projects forever.\n\nWhat is the .claude/ folder in Claude Code?\nIt's the configuration directory that Claude Code reads before doing anything else. It stores your project rules, hooks, skills, agents, commands, and settings — all in one place. Think of it as the brain behind your Claude Code setup.\n\nWhy does your CLAUDE.md file need to stay under 200 lines?\n CLAUDE.md is where you write project-specific rules for Claude to follow. Keep it under 200 lines so it stays sharp and doesn't dilute Claude's focus. It's advisory — Claude reads it, not executes it. Hooks are what actually fire every time, automatically.\n\nWhat is the difference between hooks and skills in the .claude/ folder?\n Hooks like PostToolUse.sh and SessionStart.sh are deterministic — they fire every single time, no matter what. Skills are model-invokable — Claude calls them on demand when the task requires it. Hooks automate, skills assist.\n\nHow do agents inside the .claude/ folder work?\nAgents like code-reviewer.md, researcher.md, and log-analyzer.md run in isolated context windows. This means they work independently without cluttering your main conversation — perfect for reviewing diffs, fetching web data, or parsing crash logs.\n\nHow should you use the rules/ folder for smarter Claude behavior?\nThe rules/ folder is path-scoped — files load only when Claude is working within a matching directory. For example, api.md only loads for src/api/** files. This keeps Claude's context tight and relevant instead of overwhelming it with instructions that don't apply.\n\nThis is the setup most developers skip — and wonder why their Claude results feel inconsistent.\n\n#ClaudeCode #AITools #DeveloperTools #AIProductivity\nCredits - @leadgenman"},{"href":"/p/DaT6BvdAPcm/","alt":"O Claude acabou de te dar um time inteiro de SEO!!!!!\n\nEsse sistema é open-source e roda no SEU computador, dentro do Claude Code.\nNão é \"uma ferramentinha de palavra-chave\" — é um time completo trabalhando em paralelo:\n\n🔍 Auditoria completa do seu site\n🎯 Palavras-chave que realmente vendem\n✍️ Conteúdo otimizado no padrão do Google (E-E-A-T)\n⚙️ SEO técnico, schema, SEO local, e-commerce e internacional\n\nE o pulo do gato: SEO da IA (GEO).\nHoje seu cliente pergunta pro ChatGPT antes de abrir o Google.\nEsse sistema otimiza pra você aparecer DENTRO da resposta da IA — ChatGPT, Claude e Perplexity citando o SEU site.\n\nQuem não aparece no Google E na IA, simplesmente não existe.\n\nQuer o link do sistema (open-source, de graça)?\n👉 Comenta \"SEO\" aqui embaixo que eu te mando no direct.\n\n**#claudecode #seo #opensource #marketingdigital #thenerdaiguy**"},{"href":"/p/DYrSa6PPVI2/","alt":null},{"href":"/p/DWailU-jQw5/","alt":"Most people think Claude Code is just a fancy terminal.\n\nIt's actually a 4-layer AI engineering system hiding in plain sight.\n\nLayer 1 → Project Memory (CLAUDE.md)\nLayer 2 → Behavior Gates (Hooks)\nLayer 3 → Specialized Workflows (Skills)\nLayer 4 → Team Orchestration (Agents)\n\nThe devs shipping 10x faster aren't writing better prompts.\n\nThey're building systems that stack.\n\nSave this for later 🔖\n\n#claudecode #aiengineering #softwaredevelopment #programming #artificialintelligence #agenticai #devtools #coding #techcontent #buildinpublic #softwareengineering #ai2025 #developers #codetools #llmtools"},{"href":"/p/DYDFDf0mOGQ/","alt":"This is Boris Cherny, the creator of Claude Code, and in just a few minutes, he explained how to actually master it 👀💻\n\nSave this before you forget it.\n\nHere are the 8 biggest tips:\n\n1. Start with codebase Q&A so Claude explores the project before touching anything.\n\n2. Ask about git history to understand why changes happened, not just what changed.\n\n3. Add a `CLAUDE.md` file at the project root so context loads automatically every session.\n\n4. Plan before coding. Have Claude brainstorm approaches first, then approve execution.\n\n5. Create feedback loops with tests or screenshots so Claude can verify its own work.\n\n6. Use `/memory` to inspect and control the exact context Claude is pulling in.\n\n7. Pipe logs, git status, or Sentry data into Claude through the SDK for automation workflows.\n\n8. Run multiple Claude instances in parallel using git worktrees or tmux to scale work across the codebase.\n\nThat’s the real shift.\n\nThe best AI workflows aren’t just “ask AI for code.” They’re structured systems where the model can reason, verify, and iterate with context.\n\nOnce people understand that, AI coding tools become way more powerful.\n\nWhat do you think? 🤔💬\n\n#ai #coding #claudecode #programming #technology"},{"href":"/p/DXXT6iPCNdx/","alt":null},{"href":"/p/DWDyB6UEdpl/","alt":"Claude Code、結局いくらかかるの?\n\n 「料金が複雑でよくわからない...」\n という声をよく聞くので\n  シンプルにまとめました\n\n — Claude Code 料金ガイド —\n\n  まず大前提として\n  Claude Code自体はインストール無料。\n お金がかかるのは「Claudeを動かす部分」\n つまりプラン選びが料金のすべてです。\n\n  選択肢は4つだけ\n\n  1. 無料プラン（$0）\n お試しで触ってみたい人向け\n  ただし制限がかなり厳しい\n  2. Proプラン（$20/月）\n ほとんどの人はこれでOK\n  学習用、ちょっとした業務用なら十分\n  3. Maxプラン（$100 or $200/月）\n 毎日がっつり開発する人向け\n  ほぼ上限を気にせず使える\n  4. API従量課金（使った分だけ）\n 上級者・チーム開発向け\n  常に料金を気にする必要あり\n\n  おすすめの選び方:\n まず無料で試す\n -> 気に入ったらPro\n -> 制限に引っかかったらMax\n  段階的に上げていけばOKです\n\n  コスト節約のコツ:\n /compactで会話を圧縮\n /costでこまめに確認\n  1タスク1セッションを心がける\n\n  保存して見返してね\n\n — お知らせ —\n 3/21から1ヶ月間\n  バイブコーディング個人開発ハッカソンを開催します!\n 好きなものを作りつつ\n  ClaudeCodeの活用スキルも一気にあげよう\n  詳細はプロフィールのハイライトから\n\n  #ClaudeCode #Claude #AI開発 #AIプログラミング #バイブコーディング"},{"href":"/p/DaQbsFoEdbB/","alt":"Fünf Claude Code Plugins, die kaum einer nutzt. Kein lila Gradient mehr.\n\n03 Ponytail. Lazy-Senior-Dev-Mode. 80-94 % weniger Code, 3-6× schneller.\n\n04 Higgsfield MCP. Verbindet Claude Code mit GPT Image 2, Seedance, Marketing Studio. [2m[ok | 56100ms][0m
